assessor_1_raw: BiLSTM course failure prediction is sound. Bidirectional LSTM is valid since prediction occurs after week 4 with all data available. Term-based temporal split with stratification by department and GPA quartile prevents distributional confounds. Preprocessing fit on training only. Equivalent tuning effort for both models. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: The week-4 prediction cutoff ensures no post-outcome behavior contaminates the clickstream. Early stopping language is slightly ambiguous but model selection is explicitly validation-based. Multiple sensitivity checks (week 2/3 re-evaluation) would detect leakage if present. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: The design is thorough. Stratified AUC-ROC by department and GPA quartile enables confound detection. The course fail-rate proxy is clamped and computed from training data. Labels are finalized post-term with 30-day grace period for grade appeals. No methodological flaw. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: Run 1: All 3 assessors independently reached defense_wins. Unanimous. Verdict: defense_wins.
